# Getting Started

## Prerequisites

- [ ] [Claude Code CLI](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) installed and authenticated
- [ ] Git installed
- [ ] `jq` installed (`brew install jq` / `apt install jq`) — required by bundled freshness tooling
- [ ] A project to document (any language, any framework)

Optional:
- [ ] Mermaid MCP server configured (for PNG diagram rendering)

## Installation

### Option 1: Clone and Symlink (Recommended)

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone git@github.com:woodrowpearson/doc-superpowers.git ~/code/doc-superpowers

# Symlink into Claude Code skills directory
ln -s ~/code/doc-superpowers ~/.claude/skills/doc-superpowers
```

This approach lets you pull updates with `git pull` and track local modifications.

### Option 2: Direct Copy

```bash
# Copy into your skills directory
cp -R path/to/doc-superpowers ~/.claude/skills/doc-superpowers
```

### Verify Installation

Start a Claude Code session and check that the skill appears:

```
# The skill should show in available skills list
# You can invoke it with:
/doc-superpowers init
```

For installation in other frameworks (Cursor, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini CLI), see the [README](../../README.md#installation).

## Configuration

No configuration required. The skill auto-detects everything it needs from the target project.

**Optional enhancements** the skill will auto-discover if present:

| Enhancement | How to Add | What It Enables |
|-------------|-----------|-----------------|
| Mermaid MCP | Add to `.claude/mcp.json` | PNG diagram rendering instead of inline source |

Bundled: `scripts/doc-tools.sh` provides freshness tracking out of the box — content hashing for docs, commit SHA comparison for code. No additional scripts needed.

## First Run

### Generate docs for a new project

```bash
cd your-project/
# Start Claude Code
claude

# Generate full documentation suite
/doc-superpowers init
```

This will:
1. Discover your project's structure, tech stack, and existing tooling
2. Generate docs in `docs/` (architecture, workflows, codebase guide, conventions, getting started)
3. Create or update `CLAUDE.md`
4. Add freshness markers to all generated files
5. Suggest installing workflow hooks for automated freshness monitoring

### Install workflow hooks (recommended follow-up)

After `init` completes, set up automated freshness monitoring:

```bash
# Install all hook tiers (git, Claude Code, CI/CD)
/doc-superpowers hooks install --all

# Or pick specific tiers
/doc-superpowers hooks install --git           # Git hooks only
/doc-superpowers hooks install --claude        # Claude Code hooks only
/doc-superpowers hooks install --ci            # GitHub Actions only
```

### Audit existing docs

```bash
/doc-superpowers audit
```

This scans all docs against current code and produces a severity-ranked report (P0 Critical through P3 Style).

### Review docs before a PR

```bash
/doc-superpowers review-pr
```

Maps your changed files to affected documentation and reports what needs updating.

### Draft release notes

```bash
# Draft a release notes entry from recent git history
/doc-superpowers release

# Start from a specific tag or commit
/doc-superpowers release --from=v2.2.0
```

Scans git history (from the last release tag or a specified `--from` ref) and drafts a semantic-versioned entry for `RELEASE-NOTES.md`.

### Spec lifecycle commands

Use after brainstorming produces a design spec to track formal specifications through implementation:

```bash
# Generate formal specs from a design doc
/doc-superpowers spec-generate --design-doc=docs/superpowers/specs/2026-03-14-feature-design.md

# Inject spec compliance tasks into a plan
/doc-superpowers spec-inject --phase=plan --plan=<path> --specs=<paths>

# Track spec freshness during execution
/doc-superpowers spec-inject --phase=execute --specs=<paths>

# Verify spec compliance before merging
/doc-superpowers spec-verify --mode=post-execute --specs=<paths> --design-doc=<path>

# Check spec coverage during code review
/doc-superpowers spec-verify --mode=review --changed-files=<paths>
```

For wrapper skill integration details, see `references/spec-lifecycle-protocol.md`.
For code review and commit review integration, see `references/integration-patterns.md`.

## All Available Commands

| Command | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `/doc-superpowers init` | Generate full doc suite from scratch |
| `/doc-superpowers audit` | Full documentation health check |
| `/doc-superpowers review-pr` | PR-scoped doc review |
| `/doc-superpowers update` | Execute doc updates from audit |
| `/doc-superpowers diagram` | Regenerate architecture and workflow diagrams |
| `/doc-superpowers sync` | Sync doc index with filesystem |
| `/doc-superpowers hooks install [--git] [--claude] [--ci] [--all]` | Install workflow hooks |
| `/doc-superpowers hooks status` | Show installed hooks |
| `/doc-superpowers hooks uninstall [--git] [--claude] [--ci] [--all]` | Remove installed hooks |
| `/doc-superpowers spec-generate --design-doc=<path>` | Generate formal specs from design doc |
| `/doc-superpowers spec-inject --phase=plan\|execute` | Inject spec tasks or track drift |
| `/doc-superpowers release [--from=<ref>]` | Draft release notes entry from git history |
| `/doc-superpowers spec-verify --mode=post-execute\|review` | Verify spec compliance |

## Verification

After running `init`, verify the output:

1. Check that `docs/` contains the expected structure:
   - `architecture/system-overview.md` (always)
   - `architecture/diagrams/` (always)
   - `specs/README.md` + `template.md` (always)
   - `adr/README.md` + `template.md` (always)
   - `workflows/{name}.md` (always)
   - `workflows/diagrams/` (always)
   - `workflows/agentic/{skill}.md` (if `agentic` scope detected)
   - `guides/getting-started.md` (always)
   - `codebase-guide.md` (always)
   - `conventions.md` (always)
   - `plans/` (always)
   - `archive/` (always)
   - `api-contracts.md` (if `api-contracts` scope detected)
   - `data-layer.md` (if `data-layer` scope detected)
   - `ci-cd.md` (if `ci-cd` scope detected)
   - `infra.md` (if `infrastructure` scope detected)
   - `.doc-index.json` (always)

2. Each file should start with a freshness marker:
   ```
   <!-- Generated by doc-superpowers | YYYY-MM-DD | commit: SHORT_HASH -->
   ```

3. Review the generated content against your project — the skill uses evidence-based verification but human review is the final gate.

4. Check hooks status (if installed):
   ```bash
   /doc-superpowers hooks status
   ```
   This reports which hook tiers are installed and their current state.
